title: Quality-Attribute Architecture, ADRs, and Evolvable Interfaces · Cumulative Product Review
summary: A delayed review reconstructs quality-attribute architecture, adrs, and evolvable interfaces from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for quality-attribute architecture and interface suite after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Express accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, operability, and cost as stimulus-response-measure scenarios.
Compare modular monolith, service, event, batch, managed, and buy options against explicit quality scenarios and team constraints.
Design runtime-validated APIs or events with compatibility, versioning, idempotency, pagination, error, deprecation, and consumer evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Adr Review Trigger
steps.instruction:
review case — A team proposes five services without quality scenarios, independent deployment need, compatibility policy, failure containment, or ownership capacity. The review variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.

Inspect the Adr Review Trigger trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Adr Review Trigger.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Adr Review Trigger, keep Adr Review Trigger connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Adr Review Trigger, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Adr Review Trigger, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Adr Review Trigger precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Adr Review Trigger, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A team proposes five services without quality scenarios, independent deployment need, compatibility policy, failure containment, or ownership capacity. The review variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Adr Review Trigger, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: An accepted architecture decision should not change during delivery.

stimulus.title: Adr Review Trigger case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A team proposes five services without quality scenarios, independent deployment need, compatibility policy, failure containment, or ownership capacity. The review variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: An accepted architecture decision should not change during delivery.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions. Artifact under review: quality-attribute architecture and interface suite.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, apply Adr Review Trigger without its stated preconditions, omit the quality-attribute architecture and interface suite trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Adr Review Trigger for quality-attribute architecture and interface suite.
The record names the assumption, applies Adr Review Trigger, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Adr Review Trigger record says quality-attribute architecture and interface suite looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Adr Review Trigger.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Quality Scenario
steps.instruction:
Order the Quality Scenario evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Quality Scenario.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Quality Scenario assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Quality Scenario, keep Quality Scenario connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Adr Review Trigger
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Adr Review Trigger: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A team proposes five services without quality scenarios, independent deployment need, compatibility policy, failure containment, or ownership capacity. The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.

Evidence target: Record what required effort · Review Adr Review Trigger.
steps.why: Retrieving Adr Review Trigger through the concrete quality-attribute architecture and interface suite case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Record what required effort · Review Adr Review Trigger, keep Adr Review Trigger connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.

hints: For Record what required effort · Review Adr Review Trigger, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Adr Review Trigger precondition, or invariant that must hold.

The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.

For Record what required effort · Review Adr Review Trigger, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: An accepted architecture decision should not change during delivery.
